intended_use: Candidate feature family for future A/B tests
expected_advantage: May expose periodic structure when the workload actually contains it
proof_boundary: no local proof or benchmark has been run
consumer_gate: May be used for research planning only; cannot feed routing, compression, model-quality, or performance claims without workload evidence.
consumer_policy: Consumers may cite this record for exploratory planning and blocked-axis tracking only.

non_claims: no local structural proof has been run

falsification_condition: No improvement against baselines or confounded wins after controls
fallback_substrate: ordinary dense or learned baseline remains the default
retirement_or_supersession_path: retire or narrow the substrate if controls fail, workloads do not match, or a simpler baseline dominates
